methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Wyvern is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Chris Robson, Callie Lissinna, Kurtis Broda, and Kristen Cote. The company has raised $16M to date. The comparable universe includes 5 public space and aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Given Wyvern's early-stage profile and data constraints, stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($16M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement yields $32M. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or closely-comparable private datapoints in the narrow space and aerospace cohort, despite 11 same-sector peers existing. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 25.8x median P/S multiple cannot be applied absent Wyvern's revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $27M to $37M, with a median of $32M. This range reflects typical step-up dynamics but carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method. Key risks include the stage-progression multiplier's sensitivity to market conditions and Wyvern's specific competitive position relative to better-capitalized peers. Revenue generation and clarity on addressable market focus would substantially improve conviction in any final valuation.
